query_strategy_description: 25 web-search queries grouped into 8 buckets that map to the qualitative-only entry-strategy blueprint's decision spine. Use WebSearch (native Claude tool). Avoid named-firm aggregator results; prefer regulator filings, listed-company disclosures, trade associations, government investment promotion bodies, and trade press. Compared with v1, v2 drops the dedicated capex-benchmark and opex/staffing-benchmark buckets (no quantitative modeling in the qualitative-only variant) and adds an operating-environment bucket covering regulatory climate beyond the license, cultural-fit signals, and infrastructure readiness. Compared with market_analysis, this strategy spends fewer queries on macro / sizing and more queries on qualitative mechanics: licensing path, partner identification, operating-environment read, FX/political risk.

localized_search.source_tagging: English alias only (e.g. [GSO 2024]) per L.3. Page-bottom SOURCE KEY may include original local-language source name for traceability.
localized_search.currency_hint: Fire a parallel local-currency query for sizing buckets (e.g. VND/IDR/THB/JPY/KRW) since local stats rarely report in USD.
localized_search.note: Phase M.1: dual-language search. EN baseline still runs (existing query_buckets above); this block governs the additional local-language pass.

query_buckets.purpose: One-page market opportunity context - just enough to anchor the qualitative opportunity read in the exec summary and section 06. Sizing is context, not centerpiece. Drives 06_market_opportunity_qualitative.
Identify the named target customer segment - who an entrant should chase first. Sizing where cleanly sourceable, otherwise qualitative growth direction. Drives 07_target_customer_segment.
expected_sources: national statistics bureau (BPS / GSO / NSO / PSA / DOSM / SingStat)
query_buckets.expected_tag_mix: secondary 55% / estimate 45% (segment definitions often need KIRA triangulation across multiple anchors)

query_buckets.purpose: Identify the top-5 competitors and their structural advantages an entrant must outflank, plus qualitative intensity read (fragmented / contested / consolidating / oligopoly). Drives 08_competitive_intensity_qualitative. Compressed vs market_analysis - one page only, no deep profiles.
queries: {{country}} {{industry}} top 5 players market share {{year}} revenue ranking
{{country}} {{industry}} new entrant foreign player launch {{year-2}} to {{year}}

query_buckets.purpose: Foreign ownership caps, sector licensing path, timeline-to-license bands, qualitative ongoing compliance burden. Drives 09_regulatory_licensing.
expected_sources: country investment promotion body (BKPM Indonesia / MIC Vietnam / BOI Thailand / MIDA Malaysia / EDB Singapore / BOI Philippines)
law-firm sector guides with named partners
query_buckets.expected_tag_mix: secondary 80% / estimate 20% (process timelines often quoted as ranges that need triangulation)

query_buckets.purpose: Channel mix options + qualitative per-channel read (effort to establish, reach ceiling, ramp speed) for entrants. Drives 10_distribution_gtm_channels.
queries: {{country}} {{industry}} distribution channels modern trade e-commerce traditional
{{country}} {{industry}} channel margin distributor markup retailer markup
query_buckets.expected_tag_mix: secondary 60% / estimate 40% (channel-economics data is often estimate-heavy; qualitative read is the deliverable)

query_buckets.purpose: Named JV / distributor / agent candidates. Drives 11_partnership_landscape. THIS BUCKET IS THE ONE THAT MOST DIFFERENTIATES THIS BLUEPRINT - prioritize organisation names with country footprint over generic 'partner landscape' content.
queries: {{country}} {{industry}} joint venture foreign partner local conglomerate
{{country}} conglomerates with {{industry}} subsidiary or JV partner
expected_sources: country-specific business-group rankings (Indonesia's Top 50 Family Groups / Thai conglomerate league tables / Vietnam corporate league tables)
trade press M&A and JV coverage
country chambers of commerce member directories
query_buckets.expected_tag_mix: secondary 65% / estimate 25% / primary 10% (KIRA shortlist scoring blends multiple anchors)
query_buckets.note: Use {{adjacent_industry}} to surface conglomerates that span sectors - SEA conglomerates rarely sit in one industry. Examples: Salim Group (ID) spans F&B + cement + telecom; Charoen Pokphand (TH) spans agri + retail + telecom; San Miguel (PH) spans F&B + power + infrastructure.

query_buckets.purpose: Qualitative operating-environment read for the entrant: regulatory climate stability beyond the license itself, cultural fit (B2B vs B2C norms, decision cadence, working-language reality), infrastructure readiness (logistics, digital + payment rails, talent availability for the relevant function). Drives 12_operating_environment_qualitative.
queries: {{country}} business environment FX repatriation tax stability foreign investor {{year}}
{{country}} {{industry}} cultural norms B2B decision making working language English
{{country}} logistics infrastructure digital payment rails {{industry}} readiness {{year}}
expected_sources: country investment promotion body climate reports
World Bank Doing Business / successor indicators
query_buckets.expected_tag_mix: secondary 60% / estimate 40% (qualitative tags 'tailwind' / 'neutral' / 'headwind' are KIRA synthesis)

query_buckets.purpose: FX, political, regulatory, operational, competitive-response risks to populate the qualitative 5x5 matrix. Drives 13_risk_matrix. NO quantitative severity scoring — outputs feed LOW/MED/HIGH labels.
queries: {{country}} {{currency_code}} FX volatility forecast {{year}} central bank political risk
{{country}} {{industry}} foreign company exit failed entry case studies regulatory change
expected_sources: political risk consultancies' country notes (read for facts; do not cite by firm name)
court filings on foreign-investor disputes (BANI Indonesia / SIAC Singapore)
query_buckets.expected_tag_mix: secondary 60% / estimate 40% (risk severity calls are KIRA qualitative synthesis)

query_execution_rules.parallelism: Run queries in parallel within a bucket (up to 5 concurrent). Sequential across buckets so later buckets can reference earlier findings - in particular bucket 06 (partners) benefits from knowing bucket 03 (competitive intensity) results to avoid recommending a partner that IS a competitor, and bucket 08 (risk) draws on findings from all earlier buckets.
result_filtering: Discard results from named-firm aggregators (Mordor, Frost, Euromonitor, IMARC, Synovate, Ipsos, etc.) - anti-positioning rule
Prefer results with citeable methodology disclosure
If a number appears in 3+ independent sources within +/- 10%, treat as well-anchored secondary

If a number appears in only 1 source, either find corroboration or downgrade to KIRA estimate with anchor commentary; qualitative-only register means false-precision is a bigger sin than honest ranges

For partner identification: any organisation name making the shortlist must have a verifiable corporate website, country registration, and at least one third-party reference (trade press, M&A coverage, listed-parent disclosure). No anonymous distributors.
query_execution_rules.estimate_methodology_template: Anchored estimate: {{anchor_metric}} x {{ratio}} = {{output}}. Example: 'Indonesia urban middle-class households 12-15 mn [secondary] x 30-40% category penetration band = 4-6 mn-household addressable segment [estimate]'. Capex / P&L / opex anchor templates from v1 are RETIRED — qualitative-only variant does not produce these outputs.
query_execution_rules.qualitative_synthesis_note: For market opportunity (section 06), competitive intensity (section 08), and operating environment (section 12), the output is directional language from voice_guide.md ('material', 'modest', 'contested', 'consolidating', 'tailwind', 'headwind') anchored by at most 1-2 numbers per page. Do NOT triangulate toward fully-modeled P&L scenarios — that is explicitly out of scope for v2.
query_execution_rules.research_data_output_format: Aggregate findings into a single JSON file matching the shape of references/research_data_R0152.json, with these additional top-level keys: regulator_named, partner_shortlist, operating_environment_lenses, fx_assumptions, qualitative_risk_findings. Each value tagged with source URL + access date + extraction method.

template_variable_resolution.{{currency_code}}: ISO 4217 (IDR / VND / THB / PHP / MYR / SGD)
template_variable_resolution.{{regulator_body}}: Mapped via lookup: ID->BKPM, VN->MPI/MIC, TH->BOI, PH->BOI Philippines, MY->MIDA, SG->EDB
template_variable_resolution.{{adjacent_industry}}: One- or two-step adjacent industries for partner identification: e.g. for 'roofing' use 'cement OR steel OR retail-home-improvement'; for 'fintech' use 'banking OR telecom OR retail'. Used in bucket 06 to surface multi-sector conglomerates.
template_variable_resolution.{{candidate_segment}}: Filled iteratively as bucket 02 runs - first-pass segment hypotheses (urban middle-class / SMB manufacturers / etc.)
template_variable_resolution.{{leading_player_candidate}}: Filled iteratively as bucket 03 runs - pre-known players or top-result company names
template_variable_resolution.{{year}}: Topic year (parser output, default current year)
